name: Devils Hole Pupfish
conservationEfforts: Devils Hole declared a national monument by President Truman in 1952. Supreme Court case protected water levels. Refuge populations established at Ash Meadows Fish Conservation Facility.
description: Lives in a single water-filled cavern in the Nevada desert — the smallest known habitat of any vertebrate. The visible shelf they depend on for spawning is just 3.5 x 5.5 meters.
